verses.text: 'Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands,' for His works are so glorious that all the earth shall worship and sing to His name. Praise is the world's true vocation. The greatness of God's deeds calls every nation to rejoice.

'Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands,' for His works are so glorious that all the earth shall worship and sing to His name. Praise is the world's true vocation. The greatness of God's deeds calls every nation to rejoice.

'Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands,' for His works are so glorious that all the earth shall worship and sing to His name. Praise is the world's true vocation. The greatness of God's deeds calls every nation to rejoice.

'Come and see the works of God,' who turned the sea into dry land for His people and rules by His power for ever. Past deliverances invite present faith. The God who parted the sea still reigns over all.

'Come and see the works of God,' who turned the sea into dry land for His people and rules by His power for ever. Past deliverances invite present faith. The God who parted the sea still reigns over all.

'Come and see the works of God,' who turned the sea into dry land for His people and rules by His power for ever. Past deliverances invite present faith. The God who parted the sea still reigns over all.
Bless God, ye peoples, for 'he holdeth our soul in life' and keeps our feet from slipping. Our very breath is His gift. The God who preserves us deserves continual blessing.
Bless God, ye peoples, for 'he holdeth our soul in life' and keeps our feet from slipping. Our very breath is His gift. The God who preserves us deserves continual blessing.

God has tried His people like silver, leading them through fire and water, yet brought them out 'into a wealthy place.' The furnace had a good end. God's testing refines, and leads at last to enlargement.

The psalmist comes to God's house with offerings, paying the vows his lips spoke in trouble. He keeps his promises to God. Vows made in distress are to be honoured in deliverance.

'Come and hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.' He testifies to others. Personal mercies are meant to be told for the encouragement of fellow saints.
'Come and hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.' He testifies to others. Personal mercies are meant to be told for the encouragement of fellow saints.

'If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me,' but God has heard, for He has not turned away His mercy. Cherished sin closes God's ear. The answered prayer proves a heart not clinging to evil.
